title: FileBackend: atomic write via temp-file-then-rename is the right pattern
description: FileBackend::write_secret_file uses a create_new temp file in the same directory followed by rename(). This is the only correct atomic write pattern for files. The alternatives (O_TRUNC overwrite, or writing directly to the target) leave the file in an inconsistent state if the process is killed mid-write. Using create_new (not create) with mode 0o600 guarantees the temp file always has the correct permissions rather than inheriting from a stale file. The process-id+counter naming avoids collision from concurrent put() calls.
